symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic (4-month) painless cluster of firm dome-shaped groin papules

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A months-long cluster of firm painless dome-shaped papules is the typical natural history of molluscum.
A 4-month indolent course is not how acute bacterial cellulitis behaves.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central umbilication with a whitish core on the uninflamed lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central umbilication with a keratinous/molluscum-body core is pathognomonic for molluscum contagiosum; can be appreciated by a parent on close video/photo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute peri-lesional erythema, swelling and tenderness (BOTE inflammatory flare)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute inflammation around chronic umbilicated papules is the classic BOTE immune-clearance response.
The same localised erythema/tenderness is why cellulitis must be screened for and not merely assumed away.
Absence of fever and systemic upset makes significant bacterial superinfection unlikely and supports conservative management.

symptoms.symptom_name: No deep induration, no fluctuant abscess, no lymphangitic streaking on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of induration, fluctuance and streaking argues against true cellulitis/abscess - but this is a clinician palpation finding that telehealth cannot obtain, so it is sealed and drives the safety-net rather than being available to the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Linear autoinoculation pattern along scratch marks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Linear (Koebner/autoinoculation) spread along scratch lines is characteristic of molluscum and of the child scratching.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry eczematous (molluscum dermatitis) reaction encircling the cluster

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A surrounding eczematous reaction (molluscum dermatitis) commonly accompanies molluscum and is the target of topical hydrocortisone.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic umbilicated clustered papules + acute peri-lesional inflammation + afebrile/systemically well + autoinoculation pattern together identify the benign immune-clearance phase and remove the indication for antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: No laboratory or imaging investigation required - clinical diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Inflamed molluscum (BOTE) is a clinical diagnosis; swabs, bloods and imaging are not indicated in an afebrile, systemically well child with a negative red-flag screen.
Only warranted if true secondary bacterial cellulitis/abscess develops (see safety-net); routine swabbing/antibiotics here is over-investigation and over-treatment.
